title: Nix in Combat
content:
The thing about Nix is the constant hum of activity. As if stillness was impossible for them.

The dagger slides half out of its sheath, then back with a click. Out. Back. Out. Back. Click, click, click. A metronome with no particular tempo, just the need for something to do with a hand that doesn't know how to be empty. Nix hasn't sat down since they entered the corridor. Probably hasn't sat down since this morning. The energy is low-grade and constant, like a frequency just below hearing, a skittish animal in a corridor it hasn't decided is safe yet. The head tilting, the weight shifting foot to foot, the eyes tracking everything in the room without appearing to move.

And then, somewhere ahead, something that shouldn't be there.

*Click!* The dagger stops.

Nix stands in the corridor eerily motionless and is, for one suspended moment, perfectly, completely, wrongly quiet.

That is the thing about Nix.

The enemy does not understand what it is looking at. It sees a grey-skinned thing with neon hair and an unsettling tilt to its head. It sees someone small. It makes the calculation most things make, the one that has been wrong every time. It does not understand that the fidgeting was the safety.

Nix does not telegraph. There is no inhale, no shift of the shoulders, no moment of decision. The kukris simply move, and then the thing is bleeding, and Nix is already somewhere else, already past it, already turning, the second blade catching on the backswing with the mechanical efficiency of a body that learned this somewhere it does not discuss.

The finish is close. The kukris fall.

It is not a decision. It just happens, the way breathing happens, the weapons hitting the floor and the hands doing what the hands know how to do, what they were taught to do in an arena whose name Nix does not say out loud. Claws. Teeth. The distance between a fight and its ending, closed.

Quiet.

Nix recovers a small vial almost magically from wherever they had it hidden, protected like the rarest of gems. With a practiced motion that has no hesitation in it because there has never been hesitation, not once, not in all the time they have been making this particular thing from memory they don't remember acquiring, Nix imbibes just a taste.

The warmth comes.

Something behind the eyes changes, not dramatically, not visibly to anyone watching. Just a quality of presence that was not there before. Like a window opening in a room that had seemed sealed.

Nix straightens in the aftermath and is, for a moment, approximately here.

The weight arrives with them. It always does. Old and quiet and shapeless, pressing close in the warmth the way it always presses close, and Nix does not have words for it and does not try to find them, only stands with it for the length of a breath, two, three,

*"...I know, Ember."*

A pause. The corridor settling around them. Somewhere, dripping.

*"I know."*

Nix retrieves the kukris from the ground, checks the corridor, tilts their head at a slight curious angle, and moves on as though nothing had happened.

*Click, click, click. Everything is fine.*
